review.summary: The patch correctly aligns the ACPI RHCT timebase frequency with the KVM-provided RISC-V timer frequency when KVM acceleration is active.

stages.A: Conceptually sound: RHCT should report the guest-visible timer frequency, and under KVM that comes from the host/KVM timebase rather than the fixed ACLINT default.
stages.B: The change is narrowly scoped and matches existing DT behavior by switching the RHCT frequency source based on kvm_enabled(), using hart 0 as the representative source.
stages.C: No new allocations, ownership transfers, or cleanup paths are introduced; resource-management impact is effectively nil.
stages.D: No new attack surface or privileged data exposure is introduced; the patch only changes which already-available timer frequency is encoded into firmware tables.
stages.E: No dedup issues found; the patch is a minimal correctness fix for ACPI guests on RISC-V/KVM and looks ready to merge.

disclaimer: LLM-generated draft. Not an authoritative review.
